public_training_support_route.value: Luxembourg has a public company-training co-financing route with 15% base support, and salary-cost weighting can rise to 35% for eligible participants.

workforce_context.value: In 2024 Luxembourg had 489,000 employees, and 47% were cross-border workers.

value: The uniqueness claim is false; public specialist and company-tailored French substitutes already exist.
The public co-financing route does not by itself prove this exact course is eligible.
The rounded 228k-230k worker count should not replace the raw official 489k / 47% figures.
All proposed vertical variants are not publicly proven as launch-ready.
CEFR progress reports and recordings are not externally verified as a buyer proof standard.

target_audience_summary.value: Luxembourg employers and cross-border teams that need workplace French beyond generic classroom content.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Reasonable synthesis, but still positioning rather than a default dashboard fact.

market_evidence_summary.omission_reason: Derived SEMrush market-evidence layer for prioritization and further research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into route-safe product language.

independent_operator_examples.name: Prolingua specialist language
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Commercial provider with specialist and tailor-made French offers overlapping with the product.

independent_operator_examples.name: inlingua Luxembourg
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Broad private language school serving the same general business-French lane.

independent_operator_examples.name: INLL
independent_operator_examples.evidence: Institutional provider proving category demand, but not fragmented small-operator supply.

pricing_or_offer_proof: No clean small-operator price proof was captured in the validated evidence set.
value.social_funnel_signal: Facebook search for French-in-Luxembourg terms collapsed into general Luxembourg and expat groups rather than niche French-learning communities. This supports local newcomer demand, but not a dedicated small-operator funnel for business French.
value.takeaway: Commercial supply exists, but it is led by broad schools and institutions rather than fragmented small operators.
value.why_not_stronger: Current evidence shows category demand, but not a clear long tail of niche business-French operators.

independent_operator_summary.omission_reason: Derived independent-operator signal for prioritization and further market research. Keep it out of customer-facing copy until translated into product-safe language.

product_specific_cofunding_eligibility.omission_reason: The co-financing route exists, but exact eligibility for this course is not publicly evidenced.

all_verticals_ready.omission_reason: Public evidence does not validate every proposed vertical as launch-ready.

buyer_proof_standard.omission_reason: No external source proves CEFR reports and recordings are the required buyer proof layer.
